speaker: HECTOR
text: Most putrefied core, so fair without,

speaker: HECTOR
text: Thy goodly armor thus hath cost thy life.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Now is my day’s work done. I’ll take my breath.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Rest, sword; thou hast thy fill of blood and death.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Look, Hector, how the sun begins to set,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: How ugly night comes breathing at his heels.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Even with the vail and dark’ning of the sun

speaker: ACHILLES
text: To close the day up, Hector’s life is done.

speaker: HECTOR
text: I am unarmed. Forgo this vantage, Greek.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Strike, fellows, strike! This is the man I seek.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: So, Ilium, fall thou next! Come, Troy, sink down!

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Here lies thy heart, thy sinews, and thy bone.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: On, Myrmidons, and cry you all amain

speaker: ACHILLES
text: “Achilles hath the mighty Hector slain.”

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Hark! A retire upon our Grecian part.

speaker:
A
               
              MYRMIDON
text: The Trojan trumpets sound the like, my lord.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: The dragon wing of night o’erspreads the Earth

speaker: ACHILLES
text: And, stickler-like, the armies separates.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: My half-supped sword, that frankly would have fed,

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Pleased with this dainty bait, thus goes to bed.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Come, tie his body to my horse’s tail;

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Along the field I will the Trojan trail.
